items.text_raw: falseflagsteve Urgent action is needed by the government to prevent further tragedies like this. Nice sentiment, but what exactly do you want the government to do???
items.translation_ja: falseflagsteve、こういう悲劇を防ぐには政府の緊急の対応が必要だ、ということだね。気持ちはいいけど、具体的に政府に何をしてほしいの？？？
notes_ja: 文頭の「falseflagsteve」は返信先のユーザー名で、@なしで引用している
「???」は呆れや皮肉を込めた強調
items.standard_en: Nice sentiment, but what exactly do you want the government to do?
items.author: Zaphod

items.text_raw: Murdering your own wife. Lock him up.
items.translation_ja: 自分の妻を殺すなんて。牢屋に入れろ。
notes_ja: 「Murdering your own wife.」は主語・述語のない断片的な名詞句
「Lock him up.」は口語的な命令形の短い文
items.standard_en: He murdered his own wife. Lock him up.
items.author: oldman_13

items.text_raw: by beating her with a baseball bat and stabbing her with a knife Wow. This does not sound at all like one of the mercy killings after years of taking care of a disabled partner. This sounds like pure rage. At 88 years old.... did he ever grow up?
items.translation_ja: 野球バットで殴り、ナイフで刺したということか。うわあ。これは長年介護してきた障害のあるパートナーを安楽死させる、みたいな「情け殺人」とは全然違う感じがする。純粋な怒りのようだ。88歳で……大人になれたことは一度でもあったのか？
notes_ja: 文頭「by beating...」は記事の詳細への続きで、主文が欠けている
「did he ever grow up?」は大人びていないという皮肉・侮蔑の修辞疑問
items.standard_en: By beating her with a baseball bat and stabbing her with a knife—wow. This does not sound at all like one of the mercy killings after years of taking care of a disabled partner. This sounds like pure rage. At 88 years old, did he ever grow up?
items.author: Zaphod

items.text_raw: Unbelievable! We need this to stop, these deaths are far too frequent and becoming the norm. Urgent action is needed by the government to prevent further tragedies like this.
items.translation_ja: 信じられない！こういうことは止めなければならない。こうした死は多すぎて、もはや当たり前になってきている。こういう悲劇を防ぐには政府の緊急の対応が必要だ。
notes_ja: 「We need this to stop, these deaths...」はカンマで独立した文をつなぐカンマスプライス
items.author: falseflagsteve

items.text_raw: yet again strange happenings in Japan.
items.translation_ja: またしても日本では奇妙な出来事が起きている。
notes_ja: 文頭小文字の「yet」から始まる口語的な断片
「strange happenings」はやや古めかしい／文学的な言い回し
items.standard_en: Yet again, there are strange happenings in Japan.
items.author: Slayer
